Minimum Slack Priority Rule (MS):
Define all activities with durations, precedence constraints, and resource requirements.
Compute earliest start times (EST) and latest finish times (LFT) for all activities using forward and backward passes.
Calculate slack for each activity as LFT minus EST minus duration.
Initialize current time to zero and create empty sets for scheduled, in-progress, and eligible activities.
At current time, identify eligible activities: those with all predecessors completed.
Sort eligible activities in ascending order of slack (minimum slack first).
For each activity in sorted order, check if required resources are available at current time.
Schedule activities with sufficient resources to start at current time, update resource usage, and add to in-progress set.
If no activities can be scheduled due to resource constraints, advance current time to the next completion time of in-progress activities.
Upon activity completion at current time, release its resources and remove from in-progress set.
Update eligible activities by adding those whose all predecessors are now scheduled or completed.
Repeat from step 5 (identify eligible activities at current time) until all activities are scheduled.
Latest Finish Time Priority Rule (LFT):
Define all activities with durations, precedence constraints, and resource requirements.
Compute Latest Finish Times (LFT) for all activities using a backward pass from the project deadline or critical path.
Initialize current time to zero and create empty sets for scheduled, in-progress, and eligible activities.
At current time, identify eligible activities: those with all predecessors completed.
Sort eligible activities in ascending order of LFT (earliest LFT first).
For each activity in sorted order, check if required resources are available at current time.
Schedule activities with sufficient resources to start at current time, update resource usage, and add to in-progress set.
If no activities can be scheduled due to resource constraints, advance current time to the next completion time of in-progress activities.
Upon activity completion at current time, release its resources and remove from in-progress set.
Update eligible activities by adding those whose all predecessors are now completed.
Repeat from step 4 (identify eligible activities at current time) until all activities are scheduled.
Earliest Start Time Priority Rule (EST):
Define all activities with durations, precedence constraints, and resource requirements.
Compute earliest start times (EST) for all activities using a forward pass from project start.
Initialize current time to zero and create empty sets for scheduled, in-progress, and eligible activities.
At current time, identify eligible activities: those with all predecessors completed.
Sort eligible activities in ascending order of EST (earliest EST first).
For each activity in sorted order, check if required resources are available at current time.
Schedule activities with sufficient resources to start at current time, update resource usage, and add to in-progress set.
If no activities can be scheduled due to resource constraints, advance current time to the next completion time of in-progress activities.
Upon activity completion at current time, release its resources and remove from in-progress set.
Update eligible activities by adding those whose all predecessors are now completed.
Repeat from step 4 (identify eligible activities at current time) until all activities are scheduled.
Most Successors Priority Rule (MSUC):
Define all activities with durations, precedence constraints, and resource requirements.
Compute the number of successors for each activity in the precedence network (immediate and indirect).
Initialize current time to zero and create empty sets for scheduled, in-progress, and eligible activities.
At current time, identify eligible activities: those with all predecessors completed.
Sort eligible activities in descending order of the number of successors (most successors first).
For each activity in sorted order, check if required resources are available at current time.
Schedule activities with sufficient resources to start at current time, update resource usage, and add to in-progress set.
If no activities can be scheduled due to resource constraints, advance current time to the next completion time of in-progress activities.
Upon activity completion at current time, release its resources and remove from in-progress set.
Update eligible activities by adding those whose all predecessors are now completed.
Repeat from step 4 (identify eligible activities at current time) until all activities are scheduled.
Greatest Resource Demand Priority Rule (:
Define all activities with durations, precedence constraints, and resource requirements for each resource type.
Calculate the total resource demand for each activity by summing its resource requirements across all resource types (or using a weighted sum if resource capacities differ).
Initialize current time to zero and create empty sets for scheduled, in-progress, and eligible activities.
At current time, identify eligible activities: those with all predecessors completed.
Sort eligible activities in descending order of total resource demand (greatest demand first).
For each activity in sorted order, check if required resources are available at current time.
Schedule activities with sufficient resources to start at current time, update resource usage, and add to in-progress set.
If no activities can be scheduled due to resource constraints, advance current time to the next completion time of in-progress activities.
Upon activity completion at current time, release its resources and remove from in-progress set.
Update eligible activities by adding those whose all predecessors are now completed.
Repeat from step 4 (identify eligible activities at current time) until all activities are scheduled.
Shortest Processing Time Priority Rule (:
Define all activities with durations, precedence constraints, and resource requirements.
Initialize current time to zero and create empty sets for scheduled, in-progress, and eligible activities.
At current time, identify eligible activities: those with all predecessors completed.
Sort eligible activities in ascending order of duration (shortest processing time first).
For each activity in sorted order, check if required resources are available at current time.
Schedule activities with sufficient resources to start at current time, update resource usage, and add to in-progress set.
If no activities can be scheduled due to resource constraints, advance current time to the next completion time of in-progress activities.
Upon activity completion at current time, release its resources and remove from in-progress set.
Update eligible activities by adding those whose all predecessors are now completed.
Repeat from step 3 (identify eligible activities at current time) until all activities are scheduled.
Longest Processing Time Priority Rule (L:
Define all activities with durations, precedence constraints, and resource requirements.
Initialize current time to zero and create empty sets for scheduled, in-progress, and eligible activities.
At current time, identify eligible activities: those with all predecessors completed.
Sort eligible activities in descending order of duration (longest processing time first).
For each activity in sorted order, check if required resources are available at current time.
Schedule activities with sufficient resources to start at current time, update resource usage, and add to in-progress set.
If no activities can be scheduled due to resource constraints, advance current time to the next completion time of in-progress activities.
Upon activity completion at current time, release its resources and remove from in-progress set.
Update eligible activities by adding those whose all predecessors are now completed.
Repeat from step 3 (identify eligible activities at current time) until all activities are scheduled.
